results.judge_reason: Source-domain reference describing the ondol heating system itself; no LLM/KV mapping, so functional equivalence to the proposed analogy is near-zero despite source-side vocabulary overlap.
Pure source-domain explanation of gorae channels and gudeul slabs absorbing/radiating heat — no LLM application.
Source-side overview of ondol as underfloor heating with passive thermal mass; no machine-learning/KV cache content.

Persistent Q4 (4-bit) KV cache reloaded into attention to eliminate prefill is functionally the candidate's 'low-precision summary radiates back as approximate KV' mechanism, with both keyword hits and high semantic match.
HCache restores LLM state from intermediate activations — related to state restoration but uses activations rather than low-precision KV summaries with asymmetric retention ratio.
EpiCache's episodic KV management for long conversational gaps directly addresses the 'return after long gap restore context' use case central to the candidate's gudeul-radiates-back proposal.

TRIM-KV's token-retention decay gates importance per-token rather than building a dense low-precision summary that radiates back; conceptually adjacent but a different mechanism (cited in novelty_claim).
LMCache offloads KV cache from GPU and shares it across queries but doesn't perform asymmetric low-precision radiation back into attention; explicitly cited as prior art in novelty_claim.

pipeline_reason: total_hits (2) >= 1 — Persistent Q4 KV Cache (2603.04428) implements the same low-precision KV reload mechanism, and EpiCache (2509.17396) targets the same long-conversation-gap restoration scenario; the candidate's asymmetric ondol-ratio framing is decorative rather than novel.
